character_points.note: You start with 30 CP. You gain an additional 5 CP when your character history / new-player survey is approved. More CP is earned through play.

attributes.cost_rule: To raise an attribute to level N, spend CP equal to N. (e.g. 2->3 costs 3 CP, 3->4 costs 4 CP.)

descriptions.Void: Your ability to refocus and restore spent resources. Can be exhausted to refresh Air/Earth/Fire/Water and heal all Vitality after 5 minutes of rest.

derived.vitality_note: Vitality is your physical health, equal to the total of Earth and Void divided by 2, rounded up.

armor.note: All characters may wear up to two points of physical armor. Wearing more than two points effectively requires an armor skill. Light armor = 2, Medium = 3, Heavy = 4+.

stress.note: Stress tracks psychological toll. Effects are cumulative from 1+ through 5. Not a build-time choice; tracked during play.

automatic_traits: Your Heritage (and specific sub-heritage if applicable)
Each skill header you purchase (e.g. buying the Rogue header grants the Rogue trait)
build_rules: You must purchase a Header before purchasing any skill under that header.
Open skills have no header prerequisite and may be purchased by anyone.
You cannot purchase the same skill or spell twice, even if it appears under multiple headers. A duplicated skill counts as purchased under the first header for prerequisite purposes.
Skills marked with an asterisk (*) are repeatable; pay the full cost each time. Some have a purchase limit noted in their description.
Some skills have prerequisites (other skills) listed in their descriptions.
Some headers have prerequisites, such as membership in a specific faction.
Attribute costs listed on a skill are points spent to USE the skill during play; they are refreshed each event and are NOT part of the CP build cost.
